critic_raw:
This design compares an ensemble (XGBoost on structured EHR + logistic regression on TF-IDF notes) against a logistic regression baseline on structured EHR only.

The ensemble conflates three distinct changes relative to the baseline: (1) the base learner for structured features is upgraded from logistic regression to XGBoost, (2) clinical notes are added as a new information source, and (3) an ensemble combination step is introduced. The hypothesis claims 'ensemble methods that combine historical patient treatment data with real-time clinical notes' is the operative intervention, but Model A alone (XGBoost on structured data) may already substantially outperform the logistic regression baseline regardless of note inclusion. The design does not include Model A as a standalone comparison, so it is impossible to determine whether the notes or the model upgrade is driving any observed improvement.

TF-IDF vectorization is fit on the training set (top 500 features). In clinical text, the most frequent terms are often generic stopwords or boilerplate clinical language ('patient was admitted,' 'vitals stable'). Top-500 TF-IDF over discharge summaries may capture little of the clinically specific information that distinguishes readmission risk — the feature representation may be too coarse to compete against the information already in structured features. If this is the case, the ensemble's note contribution is near-zero, but the XGBoost upgrade still produces a performance gain, and the conclusion that 'combining clinical notes improves prediction' would be spurious.

The ensemble weights are tuned on the validation set by grid search. With two base models, the weight space is one-dimensional (weight for Model A, with Model B weight = 1 - weight_A). This is low-variance tuning, but it is still an additional degree of freedom beyond either base model's own tuning. This means the ensemble has an implicit advantage from this extra optimization step that the baseline does not have.
all_issues_raised: The ensemble upgrades both the model class (LR to XGBoost on structured features) and adds clinical notes simultaneously; without a standalone XGBoost-on-structured-only arm, the note contribution cannot be isolated from the model upgrade.

TF-IDF over the top 500 terms in discharge summaries likely captures generic clinical boilerplate rather than readmission-relevant clinical specifics, meaning the note modality may contribute near-zero signal and any observed gain comes from the XGBoost component.

Ensemble combination weights are tuned on the validation set as an additional free parameter beyond either base model's hyperparameters, giving the ensemble an implicit optimization advantage the logistic regression baseline does not have.
all_issues_adjudicated: The ensemble upgrades both the model class (LR to XGBoost on structured features) and adds clinical notes simultaneously; without a standalone XGBoost-on-structured-only arm, the note contribution cannot be isolated from the model upgrade.

TF-IDF over the top 500 terms in discharge summaries likely captures generic clinical boilerplate rather than readmission-relevant clinical specifics, meaning the note modality may contribute near-zero signal and any observed gain comes from the XGBoost component.

Ensemble combination weights are tuned on the validation set as an additional free parameter beyond either base model's hyperparameters, giving the ensemble an implicit optimization advantage the logistic regression baseline does not have.
